# accessflow_datasource (Resource)

A governed database connection (relational or NoSQL). Credentials are write-only.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "accessflow_datasource" "prod_postgres" {
  name          = "prod-postgres"
  db_type       = "POSTGRESQL"
  host          = "postgres.prod.internal"
  port          = 5432
  database_name = "app"
  username      = "af_reader"
  password      = var.prod_postgres_password # write-only
  ssl_mode      = "REQUIRE"

  require_review_writes = true
  ai_analysis_enabled   = true
  review_plan_id        = accessflow_review_plan.standard.id
}

variable "prod_postgres_password" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `db_type` (String) Engine, e.g. `POSTGRESQL`, `MYSQL`, `MONGODB`, `NEO4J`.
- `name` (String) Unique datasource name within the organization.
- `ssl_mode` (String) `DISABLE`, `REQUIRE`, `VERIFY_CA`, or `VERIFY_FULL`.

### Optional

- `active` (Boolean)
- `ai_analysis_enabled` (Boolean)
- `ai_config_id` (String)
- `connection_pool_size` (Number)
- `database_name` (String)
- `host` (String)
- `jdbc_url_override` (String)
- `local_datacenter` (String) Required for Cassandra/ScyllaDB datasources.
- `max_rows_per_query` (Number)
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Write-only. Never returned by the API, so changes are applied but not detected as drift.
- `port` (Number)
- `require_review_reads` (Boolean)
- `require_review_writes` (Boolean)
- `review_plan_id` (String)
- `text_to_sql_enabled` (Boolean)
- `username` (String)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Datasource UUID.
- `organization_id` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Datasources are imported by their UUID.
terraform import accessflow_datasource.prod_postgres 11111111-2222-3333-4444-555555555555
```
